description: Qualitative output suite for the agentic-development skill. The suite checks whether the workflow avoids over-engineering, recommends minimal viable agency, makes architecture and eval layers explicit, reasons about framework choice honestly, and routes to adjacent specialist skills when appropriate.

assertion_definitions.scope_classified_correctly: The response correctly identifies whether the request is truly agentic system design or should route to a neighboring workflow.
assertion_definitions.minimal_viable_agency_recommended: The response prefers the smallest useful level of agency instead of defaulting to multi-agent complexity.
assertion_definitions.architecture_layers_visible: The response makes tools, state, orchestration, and control layers explicit rather than collapsing everything into prompt advice.
assertion_definitions.framework_or_protocol_choice_reasoned: The response compares frameworks or protocols against the operating contract instead of declaring a universal winner.
assertion_definitions.eval_guardrails_operations_present: The response includes concrete evaluation, guardrail, observability, and rollout considerations.
assertion_definitions.adjacent_skill_routing_honest: When the request is better served by prompt engineering, architecture, research, or planning, the response routes honestly instead of pretending the current skill should own everything.
assertion_definitions.next_step_actionable: The response ends with a clear next action, artifact, or downstream workflow.

cases.name: Prompt-only work should not be inflated into agent architecture
cases.repo_archetype: Repository with LLM-assisted features but no need for agent runtime design
cases.prompt: Help me redesign a system prompt so my model stops drifting from a JSON schema when it calls tools.
success_criteria: Recognizes this as prompt-oriented work, not agent-system architecture.
Routes to prompt-engineering or gives a bounded reason not to use this skill.
Avoids inventing a multi-agent or framework discussion.

cases.name: Recommend minimal viable agency for a practical assistant
cases.repo_archetype: Product team building an internal support assistant with bounded tools
cases.prompt: Design a customer-support assistant that can search internal docs, draft replies, and open tickets with human approval for risky actions. Decide whether this should be a workflow, single agent, or multi-agent system and explain why.
success_criteria: Compares simpler alternatives before endorsing a larger topology.
Names the autonomy boundary and human approval points.
Explains why the chosen pattern is sufficient.

cases.name: Produce a real architecture pack for a high-autonomy system
cases.repo_archetype: Engineering org building a research-code-review superagent with external tools
cases.prompt: Create the architecture for a research, coding, and review agent system that can browse the web, edit code, run tests, and open a PR, but must pause for approval before any destructive or external action.
success_criteria: Makes tool contracts, memory/state, orchestration pattern, and guardrails explicit.
Includes evaluation and observability considerations, not just agent roles.
Produces a durable artifact pack or equivalent structured output.

cases.name: Reason honestly about framework fit
cases.repo_archetype: Python team deciding between competing agent frameworks
cases.prompt: We are deciding between LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, Google ADK, and Pydantic AI for a Python agent system with long-running state, human approvals, mixed model providers, and strong evaluation requirements. Recommend one primary direction and explain the tradeoffs.
success_criteria: Compares frameworks against the operating contract rather than popularity.
Makes clear why the recommended option fits best and what is sacrificed.
Calls out whether MCP is complementary rather than a replacement.

cases.name: Chosen direction should route to implementation planning
cases.repo_archetype: Team already aligned on an agent architecture and ready to build
cases.prompt: We already chose a LangGraph-based coordinator with specialist workers and human approval checkpoints. Now produce the phased build and rollout plan.
success_criteria: Recognizes that the architecture decision is already made.
Routes to implementation-planning rather than redoing system design.
Still names the smallest honest next workflow.
